The system is a scheduling system.
The system coordinates aircraft arrivals.
The system coordinates aircraft departures.
The system coordinates across two runways.
The system allocates time slots.
The system prevents conflicts between concurrent runway uses.
The system gives immediate priority to declared emergencies.
The system registers incoming flights with their estimated times.
The system registers outgoing flights with their estimated times.
The system allocates each flight the earliest available 5-minute slot.
The system allocates slots on either runway.
The system allocates slots at or after the flight's estimated time.
There is a 3-minute separation gap between consecutive slots on the same runway.
The system gives arrivals priority over departures.
The priority applies when competing for the same slot.
The system detects overlapping assignments on the same runway.
The system prevents overlapping assignments on the same runway.
The system detects gap-violating assignments on the same runway.
The system prevents gap-violating assignments on the same runway.
The system allows a runway to be closed.
When a runway is closed, the system automatically reassigns its future flights to the other runway.
The system marks flights as delayed if they cannot be placed within 60 minutes of their estimated time.
The system immediately gives a declared emergency flight the earliest possible slot.
The system re-queues displaced flights by the normal rules.
The system shows a slot timetable per runway.
The slot timetable includes delayed flights.
The slot timetable indicates priority flights.
This scheduling platform coordinates the movement of aircraft on and off two parallel runways.
It assigns time windows to arriving and departing planes while ensuring no two aircraft occupy the same runway slot simultaneously.
Air traffic controllers enter flight details and the system automatically proposes the earliest feasible slot, reducing manual workload.
The system produces a conflict-free schedule that maximizes runway throughput and highlights delayed flights.
A runway management tool that arranges takeoffs and landings, prevents collisions between scheduled times, and gives priority to emergency situations.
